case.title: CITY OF COVINGTON, KENTUCKY v. PUBLIC SERV. COMM'N, KY

opinion.h1: CITY OF COVINGTON, KENTUCKY v. PUBLIC SERV. COMM'N, KY, 362 U.S. 215 (1960)
opinion.h4: CITY OF COVINGTON, KENTUCKY, v. PUBLIC SERVICE COMM'N OF KENTUCKY ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE COURT OF APPEALS OF KENTUCKY. No. 628. Decided March 21, 1960.
opinion.p: Appeal dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
Charles S. Rhyne and S. White Rhyne, Jr. for appellant.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
362 U.S. 215 SMITH ET AL. v. COLUMBIA COUNTY, OREGON, ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF OREGON. No. 645. Decided March 21, 1960.
Appeal dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
Robert Y. Thornton, Attorney General of Oregon, and Carlisle B. Roberts and Theodore W. deLooze, Assistant Attorneys General, for appellees.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
